reasoning: Trump and Putin spoke in February and March 2025 to discuss ending the war in Ukraine and establish a 30-day partial ceasefire. By late April, hopes had dimmed: Russian strikes continued, Trump voiced frustration that Putin was "tapping him along", and Trump rejected a unilateral 3-day May ceasefire proposed by Putin as insufficient. There is no evidence of a phone call having taken place in April, breaking the brief ~monthly cadence. On May 5, the Kremlin noted that a Trump-Putin meeting would be strictly "prepared accordingly" through contacts at "expert levels", suggesting that routine leader-to-leader communications are paused pending ground-level breakthroughs. Furthermore, Putin has no travel plans to the Middle East where Trump is heading in late May, precluding an imminent in-person summit and leaving phone calls as the primary direct channel if a breakthrough happens. Given the 34-day window (May 12 - June 15), the fact that talks are stalled and pushed to the 'expert level', but the overall stakes and Trump's penchant for personal diplomacy remain high, the probability of them chatting on the phone currently rests below a coin toss, around 38%.
